entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The primary crop studied, Oryza sativa L., focused on yield stability and environmental sustainability.

entities.name: NITROGEN USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ability of rice genotypes to utilize nitrogen effectively to maintain yield stability.

entities.name: NITROGEN UTILIZATION EFFICIENCY
entities.description: Variation in the efficiency of nitrogen utilization, referred to as NutE.

entities.name: CONTROL NITROGEN LEVEL
entities.description: Field condition designated as N120 used to identify high NUE genotypes.

entities.name: NITROGEN DEFICIENCY
entities.description: Field condition designated as N0 used to identify high NUE genotypes.

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE STAGE NITROGEN ASSIMILATION
entities.description: The process of nitrogen assimilatory and signalling gene expression during the reproductive stage of rice.

entities.name: NITROGEN METABOLISM AND SIGNALING GENES
entities.description: A group of genes, including NLP genes, involved in nitrogen metabolism and signaling.

entities.name: APO RICE
entities.description: An Indica rice genotype used for sequence variation analysis of nitrogen metabolism genes.

entities.name: NERICA-L-42 RICE
entities.description: A rice genotype (Oryza glaberrima*Indica) used for sequence variation analysis of nitrogen metabolism genes.

entities.name: NIPPONBARE RICE
entities.description: A japonica rice genotype used as a reference for identifying non-synonymous SNPs.

entities.name: NPF2.2
entities.description: A gene involved in nitrogen metabolism where significant non-synonymous SNPs were identified.

entities.name: PTR2
entities.description: A gene involved in nitrogen metabolism where significant non-synonymous SNPs were identified.

entities.name: NGR9
entities.description: A gene involved in nitrogen metabolism, also known as DEP1, where significant non-synonymous SNPs were identified.

entities.name: FD-GOGAT
entities.description: A gene involved in nitrogen metabolism where significant non-synonymous SNPs were identified.

entities.name: NLP3
entities.description: A nitrogen signaling gene identified as a potent genome editing target for improving NUE.

entities.name: NLP4
entities.description: A nitrogen signaling gene identified as a potent genome editing target for improving NUE.

entities.name: NLP5
entities.description: A nitrogen signaling gene identified as a potent genome editing target for improving NUE.

entities.name: NON-SYNONYMOUS SNP ANALYSIS
entities.description: The analysis of single nucleotide polymorphisms that result in amino acid sequence changes in nitrogen metabolism genes.

entities.name: GENOME EDITING
entities.description: A technique targeted at NLP3, NLP4, and NLP5 to improve nitrogen use efficiency in rice.

relationships.description: RICE is the crop for which NITROGEN USE EFFICIENCY is being improved
NITROGEN USE EFFICIENCY was identified under CONTROL NITROGEN LEVEL conditions
NITROGEN USE EFFICIENCY was identified under NITROGEN DEFICIENCY conditions
REPRODUCTIVE STAGE NITROGEN ASSIMILATION correlates to the variation in NITROGEN UTILIZATION EFFICIENCY
NITROGEN METABOLISM AND SIGNALING GENES were analyzed in APO RICE
NITROGEN METABOLISM AND SIGNALING GENES were analyzed in NERICA-L-42 RICE
NPF2.2 is one of the NITROGEN METABOLISM AND SIGNALING GENES
PTR2 is one of the NITROGEN METABOLISM AND SIGNALING GENES
NGR9 is one of the NITROGEN METABOLISM AND SIGNALING GENES
FD-GOGAT is one of the NITROGEN METABOLISM AND SIGNALING GENES
NLP3 is one of the NITROGEN METABOLISM AND SIGNALING GENES
NLP4 is one of the NITROGEN METABOLISM AND SIGNALING GENES
NLP5 is one of the NITROGEN METABOLISM AND SIGNALING GENES
NON-SYNONYMOUS SNP ANALYSIS identified SNPs in NPF2.2
NON-SYNONYMOUS SNP ANALYSIS identified SNPs in PTR2
NON-SYNONYMOUS SNP ANALYSIS identified SNPs in NGR9
NON-SYNONYMOUS SNP ANALYSIS identified SNPs in FD-GOGAT
NON-SYNONYMOUS SNP ANALYSIS identified SNPs in NLP3
NON-SYNONYMOUS SNP ANALYSIS identified SNPs in NLP4
NON-SYNONYMOUS SNP ANALYSIS identified SNPs in NLP5
NLP3 is a target for GENOME EDITING
NLP4 is a target for GENOME EDITING
NLP5 is a target for GENOME EDITING
GENOME EDITING is used to improve NITROGEN USE EFFICIENCY
NON-SYNONYMOUS SNP ANALYSIS provides genomic resources for improving NITROGEN USE EFFICIENCY
